Home»Lifestyle»Celeb News»Local Celebs»PICTURES: Akani Simbine rocks R3.7m designer watch at the Paris Olympics

Time is money— the world’s fourth-fastest sprinter, Akani Simbine, is trending after he was spotted wearing an R3.7m watch at the Olympics.

South African sprinter Akani Simbine had a stellar performance in the 100m race at the Paris 2024 Olympic Games. Akani Simbine finished fourth in the Men’s 100m final on Sunday night.

Undoubtedly, the race has since been dubbed one of the biggest highlights of the Olympics. United States sprinter Lyles won the final with thousands of seconds ahead of Jamaican sprinter Thompson and fellow countryman Kerley.

Despite finishing fourth, Akani Simbine has been said to be one of themost consistent sprinters inthe world and has proven so in more than three major track and field events over the years.

However, the South African sprinter is seemingly topping trends with his bling—a wristwatch worth $200 000. The South African athlete was spotted wearing aRichard Mille wristwatchwith a base price of R3.7 million during his 100m races at the Olympics.

According to reports, the RM 67-02 ‘Sprint Van Niekerk’ model is one of Richard Mille’s most coveted wristwatches. Surprisingly, the watch is named after South Africa’s track and field athlete, Wayde van Niekerk.

The watch boasts a light strap band and a unique bezel hand-painted in the colours of the South African flag. No doubt, Akani Simbine joins a host of athletes who have been spotted wearing the watch. Real Madrid defender Antonio Rüdiger and German Tennis playerAlexander Zverevalso own the watch.

Surprisingly, the South African athlete is part of the Richard Mille family. Akani Simbine joined the Richard Mille family at the age of 24. He isn’t the first sprinter to join this exclusive family. Jamaican athlete Yohan Blake is also a partner of the watch brand.

Speaking about hispartnership with Richard Mille,Akani Simbine said, “I am honoured to be a part of the Richard Mille family. Richard Mille strives to be the best, and I strive to be the best at what I do, which is to run extremely fast!”

However, back home, Akani Simbine joins a host of celebrities and politicians who own some of the world’s most expensive wristwatches. Former South African President Jacob Zuma reportedly owns a rare Phillippe Complication watch with a base price tag of R855 000. The watch boasts a polished 18kt white gold case.

However, earlier this year, DJ Shimza – born Ashley Raphala, hogged headlines with his Rolex Datejust 41. The watch has a base price tag of R260 000 in several certified stores. Undoubtedly, Shimza also joins an exclusive club of celebrities who own the rarest Rolex.

Tennis superstar Roger Federer wears a Rolex Datejust II. Surprisingly, he has been Rolex’s ambassador since 2012. On several occasions, Justin Bieber has also been spotted wearing his Rolex Datejust II.

However, just like DJ Shimza, Kourtney Kardashian also has the coveted Rolex Datejust 41 in her collection. TV star Jennifer Garner and David Beckham are some of the celebrities who wear the Rolex Datejust.

title: PICTURES: Akani Simbine rocks R3.7m designer watch
author: Leeroy Mukotekwa
